tool: ADSC Compliance Matrix Generator / Regulatory Precheck
coverage_note: Jurisdiction coverage is currently limited to the UN space treaties, US agency rules (FCC/FAA/NOAA), ITU references and ESA reference guidelines. National law of other launching states relevant to ADSC's adopter framing - notably Russia, China and Japan - is NOT yet covered and is future work.
stale_note: Rulepacks are versioned research artifacts (see source_date_or_version per rule). Regulations change; rulepacks can go stale and must be re-verified against current law before any real-world use.
profile_name: ADSC reference research campaign (numerical simulation only)

findings.title: Mission profile schema validation (stdlib manual validation)
findings.detail: all required fields present with expected types
findings.limitations: Manual validation dual-maintained with the schema document; unknown fields are ignored by every rule and can never produce a PASS.

findings.title: National authorization and continuing supervision of the mission
findings.authority: States Parties to the Outer Space Treaty
findings.source_reference: Outer Space Treaty, Article VI (610 UNTS 205)
findings.detail: rule does not apply to this profile
findings.limitations: Precheck only: verifies that authorization evidence is DECLARED, not that a valid authorization exists or covers this activity. Which state is the 'appropriate State Party' for a multi-state mission requires legal review.

findings.title: Registry-state jurisdiction and control: owner consent for active interference
findings.authority: States Parties to the Outer Space Treaty
findings.source_reference: Outer Space Treaty, Article VIII (610 UNTS 205)
findings.limitations: For research profiles, consent 'true' is a declared scenario assumption (ADSC spec D9), never a legal fact, and the finding notes must be read that way. The precise consent instrument (agreement, contract, note verbale) required in a real case needs legal review.

findings.title: Registration of the servicer space object
findings.authority: States Parties to the Registration Convention
findings.source_reference: Convention on Registration of Objects Launched into Outer Space, Article II (1023 UNTS 15)
findings.detail: rule does not apply to this profile
findings.limitations: Precheck of declared evidence only. Registration timing ('as soon as practicable') and the choice of registry state among joint launching states require legal review.

findings.title: Third-party liability risk allocation evidence
findings.authority: States Parties to the Liability Convention
findings.source_reference: Convention on International Liability for Damage Caused by Space Objects, Articles II-III (961 UNTS 187)
findings.detail: rule does not apply to this profile
findings.limitations: The convention binds STATES, not operators; operator-level insurance obligations come from national law and vary by jurisdiction. Amounts and scope require legal review.

findings.title: Post-mission disposal lifetime within 25 years (IADC guideline)
findings.source_reference: IADC Space Debris Mitigation Guidelines, IADC-02-01, guideline 5.3.2 (objects passing through the LEO protected region)
findings.source_date_or_version: IADC-02-01 Rev. 2 (March 2020); later revisions exist - confirm the current one
findings.limitations: Non-binding guideline. Stricter national rules supersede it where they apply - notably the US FCC 5-year rule (see FCC-ODM-5YR); under a US FCC-jurisdiction scenario the 25-year figure is NOT the operative standard. Confirm the current IADC revision before citing.

findings.title: Post-mission disposal within 5 years for LEO space stations (FCC-jurisdiction scenario)
findings.source_reference: FCC, Second Report and Order, 'Mitigation of Orbital Debris in the New Space Age', FCC 22-74 (adopted 29 September 2022); 47 CFR Part 25 space-station disposal rules
findings.detail: rule does not apply to this profile
findings.limitations: Simplified jurisdiction test (operating_state/launching_state == 'US'); the real trigger is an FCC space-station authorization, including US market access for non-US systems. Exact codified CFR subsections, transition periods and waiver practice require legal review.

findings.title: Orbital debris mitigation disclosure with the FCC application
findings.source_reference: 47 CFR 25.114(d)(14) (orbital debris mitigation disclosure for space station applications)
findings.detail: rule does not apply to this profile
findings.limitations: Precheck of declared evidence only; the disclosure's technical content (collision risk, casualty risk, maneuverability, trackability) is not evaluated here. Requires legal review.

findings.title: FAA launch and reentry authorization evidence (US commercial scenario)
findings.authority: Federal Aviation Administration, Office of Commercial Space Transportation
findings.source_reference: 51 U.S.C. chapter 509 (Commercial Space Launch Activities); 14 CFR Part 450 (Launch and Reentry License Requirements)
findings.detail: rule does not apply to this profile
findings.limitations: Precheck of declared evidence only. Whether the deorbit-kit-driven target reentry itself needs a Part 450 reentry authorization (vs. only the servicer launch) is an open legal question for ADR concepts - requires legal review.

findings.title: NOAA private remote-sensing system authorization evidence
findings.authority: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commercial Remote Sensing Regulatory Affairs
findings.source_reference: 51 U.S.C. chapter 601 (Land Remote Sensing Policy); 15 CFR Part 960 (Licensing of Private Remote Sensing Space Systems)
findings.detail: rule does not apply to this profile
findings.limitations: Placeholder rule: the US-nexus test (US person / platform control) and the 2020 tiering system are NOT modelled - the rule fires on any remote-sensing payload. Rendezvous inspection cameras may or may not fall under Part 960. Requires legal review.

findings.title: Frequency coordination / notification filing evidence for the RF payload
findings.authority: International Telecommunication Union (via the responsible national administration)
findings.source_reference: ITU Radio Regulations, Article 9 (coordination) and Article 11 (notification and recording)
findings.detail: rule does not apply to this profile
findings.limitations: Placeholder rule: ITU obligations run through the responsible national administration, and band-specific coordination requirements are NOT modelled. The Radio Regulations bind administrations, not operators directly. Requires review by the responsible administration / legal counsel.

findings.title: ESA space debris mitigation: LEO clearance reference (5-year class)
findings.source_reference: ESA Space Debris Mitigation Requirements, ESSB-ST-U-007, Issue 1 (30 October 2023)
findings.detail: rule does not apply to this profile
findings.limitations: ESSB-ST-U-007 applies contractually to ESA-procured missions; it is cited here as a reference for where European practice is heading, not as a legal requirement on third parties. Clause-level applicability requires review.

findings.title: Zero Debris alignment statement (voluntary charter)
findings.authority: European Space Agency and Zero Debris community
findings.detail: No Zero Debris alignment statement is declared (informational: the charter is voluntary; an ADR evidence package benefits from stating its position).; evidence_documents: does not contain 'zero_debris_alignment_statement'
findings.limitations: Voluntary charter, not a legal requirement. Informational finding only.

findings.title: No unconsented active interference with a registered space object (ADSC policy)
findings.source_reference: ADSC specification v4.2, decisions D9 and D11 (adsc-specification-v4.md)
findings.limitations: Internal policy, stricter than a legal minimum by design. For research profiles the consent flag is a declared scenario assumption (D9).

findings.title: No live-ephemeris ingestion and no target-specific operational products (ADSC policy)
findings.source_reference: ADSC specification v4.2, decision D11 and hard rule R13 (adsc-specification-v4.md)
findings.limitations: Internal policy. Either flag alone fails the rule; a missing flag evaluates UNKNOWN, never PASS.

findings.title: Non-weaponization: research-only or consented, class-level use (ADSC policy)
findings.source_reference: ADSC specification v4.2, section 1 and decision D11; README peaceful-use disclaimer
findings.limitations: Proxy check: 'non-weaponization' is not directly observable from profile fields; the rule uses research-only / consent / no-targeting-products as the observable proxy. Internal policy, not law.

findings.title: Export-control review status (ITAR/EAR precheck placeholder)
findings.authority: ADSC project policy (referencing US ITAR/EAR as the typical regime)
findings.source_date_or_version: snapshot 2024-era regime structure; regimes change - re-verify
findings.detail: Export-control review is not complete. Open-literature methods (spec D11) are the current basis for research distribution, but a documented review is required before sharing controlled technical data or starting hardware/flight work.
findings.limitations: Placeholder: jurisdiction-specific export regimes (US ITAR/EAR cited as the typical case) are not modelled in detail. Requires export-control counsel review.

findings.title: WP5 campaign compliance metadata consistent with this mission profile
findings.source_reference: generated/wp5_campaign_runs.csv compliance columns (schema 1.0); ADSC specification v4.2, D9/D11
findings.detail: WP5 campaign metadata consistent with research profile; all 7 mapped columns + 2 policy columns consistent across 1000 runs
findings.limitations: Consistency check between two research artifacts; it makes no statement about the real world. If the WP5 CSV is absent the result is UNKNOWN, never PASS.
